title: Main Control Board Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: The F1 E1 error code indicates the dishwasher's main electronic control board has experienced an internal communication or memory error. The board cannot properly execute cycle commands. A power reset may clear a temporary glitch, but a persistent error typically requires board replacement.

causes.cause: Loose wiring connection to control board

symptoms: Dishwasher does not respond to controls
Cycle does not start
Error code displayed or lights flashing
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Turn it back on and attempt to run a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dishwasher is receiving proper voltage (120V AC) at the junction box under the unit.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Remove the inner door panel to access the control board. Check all wire harness connections are firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Replace main control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, replace the main control board. Disconnect all harnesses, remove mounting screws, and install the new board.

whenToCallPro: If the power reset does not clear the error, professional diagnosis is recommended before purchasing an expensive replacement board.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F1E1 control board error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F1E1?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage the control board. Consider using a surge protector for the dishwasher circuit.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the control board?
faq.a: If the dishwasher is less than 7 years old, a control board replacement is usually more cost-effective than a new unit.

faq.q: How do I reset my Amana dishwasher?
faq.a: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then turn it back on. This performs a full reset.

title: EEPROM Memory Error
shortDescription: The control board cannot access its stored programming.
fullDescription: The F1 E2 error code indicates the main control board's EEPROM memory has failed or become corrupted. The EEPROM stores cycle programming and configuration data. Without this data, the dishwasher cannot run any cycles.

causes.cause: Corrupted EEPROM data from power interruption

symptoms: Dishwasher does not start any cycle
Error flashes on display at power-on
Buttons may beep but cycles do not begin
Random light patterns on control panel

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes to fully discharge the board. Turn it back on and test.

fixSteps.title: Try a second reset
fixSteps.instruction: If the first reset fails, turn the breaker off for 30 minutes and try again. Some EEPROM issues can clear with an extended power-off period.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, remove the door inner panel, and visually inspect the control board for burn marks, corrosion, or damaged components.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: The EEPROM cannot be reprogrammed. Replace the entire main control board with the correct part for your model.

whenToCallPro: This error almost always requires board replacement. Professional diagnosis can confirm before you purchase the part.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F1E2 EEPROM error fix

faq.q: Can the EEPROM be reprogrammed?
faq.a: No, the EEPROM cannot be reprogrammed by consumers. The entire control board must be replaced.

faq.q: What causes EEPROM failure?
faq.a: Power surges, electrical noise, and age are the most common causes of EEPROM failure.

faq.q: Is F1E2 the same as F1E1?
faq.a: Both indicate control board issues, but F1E2 specifically relates to memory while F1E1 is a general board error.

title: User Interface Communication Error
shortDescription: The control panel cannot communicate with the main control board.
fullDescription: The F2 E1 error code indicates a communication breakdown between the user interface (button panel) and the main electronic control board. The two boards are connected by a ribbon cable or wire harness that may be loose or damaged.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable or wire harness

symptoms: Buttons do not respond
Display is blank or partially lit
Dishwasher will not start
Lights flash in an unusual pattern

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Open the dishwasher door and remove the inner door panel screws. Carefully access the ribbon cable between the UI and main board. Disconnect and firmly reseat both ends.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the connectors and boards for signs of moisture, corrosion, or steam damage. Dry any moisture with a soft cloth.

fixSteps.title: Replace the UI board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating does not fix the issue, replace the user interface board. Disconnect the harness, remove the mounting screws, and install the new board.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the UI board does not resolve the error, the main control board may be faulty and requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F2E1 communication error fix

faq.q: Can steam cause F2E1?
faq.a: Yes, steam from hot wash cycles can cause moisture on connectors. The door gasket should be checked for proper seal.

faq.q: Should I replace both boards?
faq.a: No, start with reseating the cable, then try replacing the less expensive UI board first.

faq.q: How do I access the control boards?
faq.a: Open the dishwasher door and remove the screws along the inner edge of the door panel. The boards are located inside the door.

title: User Interface Board Error
shortDescription: The user interface control panel has an internal fault.
fullDescription: The F2 E2 error code indicates the user interface board itself has experienced an internal failure. Unlike F2E1 which is a communication error, F2E2 means the UI board has detected a fault in its own circuitry, such as a stuck button or failed component.

causes.cause: Stuck or shorted button on control panel
Wiring fault to the UI board

symptoms: Buttons unresponsive or erratic
One button appears stuck or constantly pressed
Display shows wrong information
Dishwasher starts by itself

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck buttons
fixSteps.instruction: Press each button on the control panel firmly and release. Check if any buttons feel stuck or do not spring back properly.

fixSteps.title: Clean the control panel
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the control panel with a slightly damp cloth to remove any residue or moisture around the buttons that could cause short circuits.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then turn it back on to reset the board.

fixSteps.title: Replace UI board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the UI board is likely faulty. Disconnect power, remove the door inner panel, disconnect the harness, and install a new UI board.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the UI board does not clear the error.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F2E2 UI board error fix

faq.q: Can a stuck button cause F2E2?
faq.a: Yes, a stuck button is a common cause. Try pressing each button to free any that may be stuck.

faq.q: Is the UI board expensive to replace?
faq.a: The UI board for Amana dishwashers typically costs between $40 and $120 depending on the model.

faq.q: Can water damage cause F2E2?
faq.a: Yes, steam or splash damage can affect the UI board. Ensure the door seal is intact to protect the electronics.

title: Slow Water Fill
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not filling with water fast enough.
fullDescription: The F8 E1 error indicates the dishwasher did not fill with enough water within the allowed time. The water inlet valve may not be opening, the water supply may be restricted, or the water level sensor is not detecting adequate water in the tub.

causes.cause: Water supply valve turned off or partially closed

symptoms: Dishwasher starts but does not fill with water
Error displayed early in the wash cycle
Dishes come out dirty and dry
No water spray sounds during cycle

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water supply valve under the sink. Ensure it is fully open by turning it counterclockwise.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the water line from the inlet valve (located under the dishwasher). Remove and clean the small mesh screen inside the valve port.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged, use a multimeter to test the inlet valve solenoid coil (should read 500-1500 ohms). Also check that the valve gets power during fill by testing voltage at the connector.

fixSteps.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve coil is open or the valve does not open when powered, replace it. Disconnect power and water, remove the lower access panel, disconnect the water line and wiring, and install the new valve.

whenToCallPro: If the water supply and inlet valve are functioning properly but the error persists, the float switch or control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F8E1 water fill error fix

faq.q: Why is my dishwasher not filling with water?
faq.a: The most common causes are a closed water supply valve or a clogged inlet screen. Check both of these first.

faq.q: Where is the water inlet valve on my Amana dishwasher?
faq.a: The inlet valve is located behind the lower access panel at the front of the dishwasher, where the water supply line connects.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause F8E1?
faq.a: Yes, if your home water pressure is below 20 PSI, the dishwasher may not fill fast enough. Check with your water supplier.

title: Overflow / Too Much Water
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected too much water in the tub.
fullDescription: The F8 E4 error code means the dishwasher float switch has triggered an overflow condition. Water in the tub has risen above the maximum safe level. The dishwasher will activate the drain pump to remove excess water. This is commonly caused by a stuck-open inlet valve or a stuck float switch.

causes.cause: Faulty control board not closing inlet valve

symptoms: Water leaking from bottom of dishwasher
Dishwasher runs drain pump continuously
Excessive water visible in the tub
Error displayed and cycle stops

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the water supply valve under the sink to stop water from entering the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher and locate the float assembly on the bottom of the tub (a small dome or cylinder). Push it up and down to ensure it moves freely. Remove any debris blocking it.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher off and unplugged, turn on the water supply. If water continues to enter the tub, the inlet valve is stuck open and must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water. Remove the lower access panel, disconnect the water line and wiring from the valve, remove the mounting screws, and install the new valve.

whenToCallPro: If the float switch and inlet valve appear to be working but the error persists, a professional should inspect the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F8E4 overflow error fix

faq.q: Is the F8E4 overflow dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, excess water can damage your floors and cabinets. Turn off the water supply immediately when this error appears.

faq.q: Where is the float switch in my dishwasher?
faq.a: The float switch is located on the bottom of the dishwasher tub, usually in the front-left corner. It is a small dome-shaped component.

faq.q: Can food debris cause F8E4?
faq.a: Yes, food particles can get under the float mechanism and prevent it from moving freely. Clean around the float regularly.

title: Long Drain Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is taking too long to drain water.
fullDescription: The F9 E1 error code means the dishwasher has been trying to drain water but the water level has not dropped to the expected level within the time limit. The drain pump may be blocked, the drain hose may be clogged, or the garbage disposal knockout plug may not have been removed during installation.

causes.cause: Clogged drain hose or garbage disposal connection
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed
Blocked drain pump or sump filter

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle
Drain pump runs but water does not drain
Water backs up into the tub

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose under the sink for kinks or clogs. The hose should rise to connect at an air gap or high loop before connecting to the garbage disposal or drain.

fixSteps.title: Check the garbage disposal
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal to clear any blockage. For new installations, ensure the knockout plug inside the disposal has been removed.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and the filter assembly from the bottom of the tub. Clean all parts under running water to remove food debris and grease.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the drain hose and filter are clear, the drain pump may be blocked or failed. Remove the sump cover and check for debris in the pump impeller. Replace the pump if the motor does not run.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor is not running and needs replacement, or if you cannot locate the blockage.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F9E1 drain error fix

faq.q: Why is there standing water in my Amana dishwasher?
faq.a: Standing water usually means the drain hose is clogged, the garbage disposal is blocked, or the drain pump has failed.

faq.q: What is the knockout plug?
faq.a: When a dishwasher connects to a garbage disposal, a plastic plug inside the disposal must be knocked out with a screwdriver and hammer. If not removed, the dishwasher cannot drain.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dishwasher filter?
faq.a: Clean the filter assembly at least once a month, or more often if you do not pre-rinse dishes.

title: Water Heating Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher water is not reaching the target temperature.
fullDescription: The F6 E1 error code indicates the water in the dishwasher is not heating to the required temperature within the allowed time. The heating element or the temperature sensor (thermistor) may be faulty, or the incoming water temperature may be too low.

causes.cause: Control board not powering the heater

symptoms: Dishes not clean or sanitized properly
Water feels cold during cycle
Cycle takes much longer than normal
Error appears during main wash phase

fixSteps.title: Run hot water at the sink first
fixSteps.instruction: Before starting the dishwasher, run the hot water faucet at the nearest sink until the water is hot. This ensures hot water reaches the dishwasher from the start.

fixSteps.title: Check water heater temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your home water heater is set to at least 120 degrees Fahrenheit. The dishwasher needs incoming water at 120F to work properly.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element under the tub or through the lower access panel. Use a multimeter to test for continuity (should read 10-30 ohms). Replace if open.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the thermistor (temperature sensor) on the tub. Disconnect it and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 50,000-55,000 ohms. Replace if the reading is significantly different.

whenToCallPro: If the heating element and thermistor test good, the control board relay may be faulty and requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F6E1 water heating error fix

faq.q: What temperature should the water be?
faq.a: The dishwasher needs incoming water at 120 degrees Fahrenheit. The dishwasher heater boosts it further for sanitize cycles.

faq.q: Can cold water damage my dishwasher?
faq.a: Cold water will not damage the dishwasher, but dishes will not be cleaned properly and the F6E1 error may appear.

faq.q: Should I use the heated dry option?
faq.a: Heated dry is optional but uses the same heating element. If the heater has failed, neither heating function will work.

title: Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: The F6 E2 error code indicates the dishwasher thermistor (temperature sensor) is reading an open or short circuit. The control board cannot determine the water temperature, so it cannot regulate heating properly. The thermistor needs to be tested and likely replaced.
symptoms: Water does not heat or overheats
Error displayed during wash cycle
Dishes not cleaned properly
Cycle may not complete

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then turn it back on and run a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Locate the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: The thermistor is typically located on the lower part of the tub interior or near the sump area. It is a small sensor with two wires.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and the thermistor wires. Test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature, it should read approximately 50,000-55,000 ohms. An open or zero reading means it has failed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the defective thermistor from its mounting location. Install the new thermistor and reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the thermistor does not fix the error, the control board thermistor input circuit may be faulty.

faq.q: What does the thermistor do?
faq.a: The thermistor measures the water temperature so the control board can regulate heating during wash and sanitize cycles.

faq.q: Is the thermistor the same as a thermostat?
faq.a: No. A thermistor continuously measures temperature with variable resistance. A thermostat opens or closes at a set temperature.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without a thermistor?
faq.a: No, the dishwasher will not run properly without temperature feedback. The F6E2 error will prevent normal operation.

title: Wash Pump Motor Error
shortDescription: The wash pump motor is not running or has failed.
fullDescription: The F3 E1 error code indicates the dishwasher's main wash pump motor is not operating correctly. The control board is sending power to the motor but is not detecting that it is running. This results in no water circulation and no cleaning action during the wash cycle.

causes.cause: Control board not providing power to pump

symptoms: No water spraying during wash cycle
Dishes come out dirty
Humming sound from pump area
Error appears during wash phase

fixSteps.title: Check for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly. Check for any debris, broken glass, or food particles that may be jamming the pump impeller.

fixSteps.title: Attempt to free the pump
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, reach into the sump and try to rotate the pump impeller by hand. Remove any obstructions found.

fixSteps.title: Check motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the pump motor under the dishwasher by removing the lower access panel. Check the wiring connections to the motor for loose or burned terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wash pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor does not run with clear impeller and proper wiring, replace the wash pump motor assembly. Disconnect the water lines, wiring, and mounting hardware.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor needs replacement, as it involves accessing the underside of the dishwasher and disconnecting plumbing.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dishwasher F3E1 wash pump error fix

faq.q: Can broken glass jam the wash pump?
faq.a: Yes, broken glass is a common cause of pump jams. Always check for and remove any broken glass fragments.

faq.q: How do I know if the pump motor is bad?
faq.a: If the impeller is clear and wiring is good but the motor does not run or only hums, the motor has likely failed.

faq.q: Can I hear the pump running?
faq.a: Yes, you should hear the pump during the wash cycle. If you only hear humming or silence, the pump may be jammed or failed.
